purpose: Registry for WaveSight AI V2 daily source collection, interface status, evidence role, fallback path, and automation eligibility.

source_levels.A: high-quality commercial media or authoritative research
source_levels.B: industry, ecosystem, marketplace, developer, funding, or curated vertical source
source_levels.C: community, social, aggregator, or lead source
source_levels.M: mixed acquisition channel; classify the resolved original source per item

classification_policy.rule: Source level classifies the resolved original source, not necessarily the acquisition channel.
classification_policy.mixed_channel_rule: AI HOT, follow-builders and similar channels are discovery mechanisms. Their collected items must keep original_url/source_url and receive S/A/B/C evidence level after resolving the original source.
classification_policy.front_signal_rule: Front Signal source counts use resolved original S/A/B evidence, not the aggregator channel itself.

daily_collection_policy.rule: Acquisition channels can expand coverage, but evidence level is assigned to the resolved original source. Every Front Signal needs at least 3 resolved S/A/B sources. Expanded Raw volume must not lower publishing thresholds.
daily_collection_policy.aihot_policy: AI HOT is one of three co-primary V2 daily discovery lanes. It provides high-volume trend density and Chinese AI ecosystem coverage. AI HOT items must still be traced back to original source URLs before becoming Signal evidence.
daily_collection_policy.follow_builders_policy: follow-builders is one of three co-primary V2 daily discovery lanes. It captures builder, founder, operator and engineering-practice viewpoints. Builder posts may become Point material or lead to S/A/B verification, but the channel itself is not counted as a fact source.
daily_collection_policy.keyword_search_policy: Network keyword search is WaveSight's own active monitoring lane. It covers funding, product launches, customers, regulation, counter-evidence and commercial facts. Search results must be resolved to original S/A/B sources before becoming Front Signal evidence.

sources.name: GDELT DOC 2.0
sources.rate_limit_note: public endpoint; unknown hard limit; use low-frequency daily queries
sources.query_scope: Global AI, enterprise AI, AI agent, AI startup, regulation, procurement and funding news in 24h/72h windows.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to search-only queries against S/A media and official company pages; record GDELT outage in source probe report.
sources.risk_note: Broad global coverage but noisy; must dedupe syndicated articles and recover original reporting or first-party source.

sources.name: AI HOT selected items
sources.rate_limit_note: public API states 600 req/min/IP; daily automation should use serial low-volume requests
sources.query_scope: Chinese curated AI news leads in selected mode, especially model releases, products, industry, papers, tips and builder posts.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to original item URL, official source, GDELT/HN/GitHub search, or manual review. Do not use AI HOT summary as primary evidence.
sources.risk_note: Curated acquisition channel. Do not assign one fixed evidence level to the channel; classify each resolved original source as S/A/B/C after opening the item URL.

sources.name: follow-builders monitored builder feed
sources.rate_limit_note: local skill / configured sources; depends on upstream platform availability
sources.query_scope: Builder posts, product demos, technical opinions, practice notes, disagreements and early signals from monitored AI builders.
sources.fallback_path: Use original post URL as Point material, then verify factual claims through official blogs, docs, GitHub, product pages or A-level reporting.
sources.risk_note: Small curated builder sample of about 25 monitored builders, usually producing around 10-20 usable daily items. Fully scan it, but do not assign a fixed Raw or Pool quota. It can feed Point and trigger verification, but the channel itself is not fact-main evidence.

sources.name: Hacker News Algolia Search
sources.rate_limit_note: public search API; use serial requests
sources.query_scope: Developer-side discussion of AI agent, AI coding, open source model, inference, evals, security and startup launches.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to Hacker News Firebase item API or original linked URL; require S/A/B verification before Signal use.
sources.risk_note: Community signal only; votes and comments are not factual proof.

sources.name: Hacker News Firebase API
sources.rate_limit_note: public Firebase endpoint; use small top/new item fetches
sources.query_scope: Top/new developer stories that may point to AI infrastructure, open source releases or startup launches.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to HN Algolia search and original story URL.
sources.risk_note: Requires filtering; C-level trigger source only.

sources.name: GitHub REST Search API
sources.rate_limit_note: unauthenticated requests are limited; authenticated token can raise limit but is not required for MVP
sources.query_scope: New or fast-growing AI agent, AI coding, evals, model routing, MCP, local inference, security and workflow repositories.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to GitHub web search and project README manual review; require adoption/commercial evidence before Front Signal.
sources.risk_note: Stars alone are weak evidence; watch for demos, forks and duplicate forks.

sources.name: arXiv API
sources.rate_limit_note: public API; keep request interval modest
sources.query_scope: AI agent, model routing, evals, multimodal, robotics, on-device AI, inference optimization and safety research.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to arXiv RSS, Semantic Scholar search or manual review; do not turn research claims into commercial facts without adoption evidence.
sources.risk_note: Research is early evidence; commercial relevance must be separately established.

sources.name: OpenAI official news, docs and changelog
sources.rate_limit_note: public web/RSS where available; unknown limit
sources.query_scope: Model/API/product releases, pricing, developer docs, safety, enterprise and customer proof.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to official docs search, GitHub/openapi pages, or manual official-site review.
sources.risk_note: First-party source; still distinguish launch claims from customer adoption.

sources.name: Anthropic news, research and docs
sources.rate_limit_note: public web/RSS where available; unknown limit
sources.query_scope: Claude, Claude Code, enterprise, safety, research, model behavior and developer product changes.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to Anthropic docs, GitHub releases or manual official-site review.
sources.risk_note: First-party source; separate research from commercially deployed product evidence.

sources.name: Google AI / DeepMind official updates
sources.rate_limit_note: public web; unknown limit
sources.query_scope: Gemini, DeepMind research, product integration, AI developer tooling and enterprise AI changes.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to Google AI blog, Google Cloud blog or official docs.
sources.risk_note: First-party source; product availability often varies by region and customer tier.

sources.name: Microsoft AI and Azure AI official blogs
sources.rate_limit_note: public web/RSS where available; unknown limit
sources.query_scope: Copilot, Azure AI, enterprise adoption, agent governance, security, marketplace and customer deployments.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to Azure updates, Microsoft Learn or official customer stories.
sources.risk_note: First-party source; check whether announcements are preview, GA or customer story.

sources.name: GitHub Blog AI
sources.rate_limit_note: public blog; unknown limit
sources.query_scope: Copilot, agentic workflows, developer productivity, code review, AI coding, enterprise engineering changes.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to GitHub changelog, GitHub docs or repo releases.
sources.risk_note: First-party developer ecosystem source; quantify business impact carefully.

sources.name: NVIDIA AI Blog
sources.rate_limit_note: public blog; unknown limit
sources.query_scope: AI infrastructure, enterprise adoption, robotics, inference, accelerators, partnerships and deployment stacks.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to NVIDIA newsroom, developer docs or partner announcement.
sources.risk_note: First-party infrastructure source; watch for partner PR inflation.

sources.name: TechCrunch AI
sources.rate_limit_note: public RSS/web; unknown limit
sources.query_scope: AI startup, funding, product launches, regulation, platform changes and venture trends.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to company announcement, investor post, SEC/press release or GDELT search.
sources.risk_note: High-quality media but still secondary; recover original source before high-confidence Signal.

sources.name: Reuters AI search
sources.rate_limit_note: public web; may require search/manual review
sources.query_scope: Major AI regulation, corporate adoption, markets, legal, infrastructure and geopolitical news.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to GDELT, company filing or official regulator/court source.
sources.risk_note: Strong secondary source; public access can vary by region and page.

sources.name: VentureBeat AI
sources.rate_limit_note: public web/RSS; unknown limit
sources.query_scope: Enterprise AI, agent platforms, model releases, funding and infrastructure adoption.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to official company, funding announcement or product docs.
sources.risk_note: Useful for enterprise AI, but distinguish sponsored or vendor-driven content.

sources.name: Product Hunt API v2
sources.rate_limit_note: requires developer token; not enabled by default
sources.query_scope: Early AI products, launches, user-facing experiments and category clusters.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to public Product Hunt pages or manual review. Do not require this API for daily success.
sources.risk_note: Requires token and terms review; launch popularity is not proof of commercial traction.

sources.name: Y Combinator companies public directory
sources.rate_limit_note: public web; use low-frequency manual or search-only checks
sources.query_scope: New AI startup clusters, batch themes, early vertical AI products and founder/customer positioning.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to startup site, founder announcement, Demo Day coverage or investor post.
sources.risk_note: Startup directory is early signal; needs product/customer/funding verification.

sources.name: a16z AI and enterprise blog
sources.rate_limit_note: public web/RSS where available; unknown limit
sources.query_scope: Investment theses, market maps, portfolio announcements and AI company formation signals.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to portfolio company announcement, other investor blog or funding media.
sources.risk_note: Investor perspective has incentives; use as thesis or funding support, not standalone fact-main.

sources.name: Sequoia AI posts
sources.rate_limit_note: public web; search-only/manual review
sources.query_scope: AI market theses, portfolio announcements, early investment directions and founder interviews.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to portfolio company site, funding database or media coverage.
sources.risk_note: Investor narrative; confirm with company/funding source.

sources.name: AWS Marketplace AI listings
sources.rate_limit_note: public web; no scraping burst
sources.query_scope: Enterprise AI products, security, agents, data platforms and procurement-ready listings.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to vendor marketplace pages, product docs or customer case.
sources.risk_note: Listing shows distribution/procurement path, not necessarily revenue.

sources.name: Azure Marketplace AI listings
sources.rate_limit_note: public web; no scraping burst
sources.query_scope: Enterprise AI, copilots, security, analytics, data and procurement-ready AI solutions.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to vendor page, Microsoft partner page or customer proof.
sources.risk_note: Listing evidence must be combined with adoption/customer signal.

sources.name: Hugging Face papers and model ecosystem
sources.rate_limit_note: public web/API options; unknown limit
sources.query_scope: Open models, quantization, local inference, agent frameworks and adoption by developers.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to arXiv, GitHub repo, model card or project page.
sources.risk_note: Model popularity is weak evidence; confirm license, deployment and productization.

sources.name: Reddit API / AI subreddits
sources.rate_limit_note: OAuth and platform policy required; not enabled by default
sources.query_scope: User pain, adoption stories, complaints, workflows and counter-evidence in AI communities.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to public web search snippets or manual review; C-level only.
sources.risk_note: Requires platform policy review. Community comments are leads, not facts.

sources.name: X / Twitter builder posts
sources.rate_limit_note: official API requires account/key; non-official scraping not enabled
sources.query_scope: Builder product releases, practice feedback, demos, non-consensus views and early tool adoption.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to existing approved builder pipeline, original official blog, GitHub or product page.
sources.risk_note: Do not enable unofficial scraping without explicit user consent. Treat as Point/lead source only.

sources.name: LinkedIn company and founder posts
sources.rate_limit_note: official access requires approval; not enabled by default
sources.query_scope: Enterprise customer announcements, founder/product updates, hiring and partnership signals.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to company blog, press release, customer page or manual review.
sources.risk_note: Do not use non-official scraping by default; requires user approval.

sources.name: Crunchbase API
sources.rate_limit_note: paid/API approval required; not enabled by default
sources.query_scope: Funding rounds, investors, company metadata and category clusters.
sources.fallback_path: Fallback to company/investor announcement, TechCrunch, PRNewswire or public filings.
sources.risk_note: Paid/approved data source. Do not hard-code as required.
